title: Cambios entre versiones de la base de plantas endemicas del Peru
text: Las tablas ppendemic_tab14, ppendemic_tab15 y ppendemic_tab16 representan extracciones sucesivas de WCVP. Compararlas permite identificar: nombres que permanecen entre versiones; posibles inclusiones y exclusiones; correcciones ortograficas o de terminacion; cambios de rango infraespecifico; y posibles transferencias entre generos. Una inclusion o exclusion observada implica necesariamente que una especie haya sido descubierta o dejado de ser endemica. Tambien puede reflejar cambios taxonomicos, nomenclaturales o de los criterios de distribucion usados por WCVP. Por eso, esta viñeta distingue los cambios observados de su posible interpretacion.

title: Cambios entre versiones de la base de plantas endemicas del Peru
text: La unidad de comparacion es taxon_name. Los nombres presentes solo en la version nueva son inclusiones observadas y los presentes solo en la version anterior son exclusiones observadas. Para detectar posibles reemplazos se calcula una puntuacion conservadora entre cada nombre retirado y cada nombre añadido. La puntuacion combina similitud textual y coincidencias en epitetos, autoria, familia y año de publicacion. Estos vinculos son candidatos para revision, sinonimias confirmadas.
code: candidate_replacements <- function(removed, added) {   if (nrow(removed) == 0L || nrow(added) == 0L) {     return(tibble::tibble())   }    same_val <- function(x, y) {     !is.na(x) & !is.na(y) & x != "" & y != "" & x == y   }    name_sim <- function(x, y) {     distance <- diag(adist(tolower(x), tolower(y)))     lengths <- pmax(nchar(x), nchar(y))     1 - distance / lengths   }    pairs <- dplyr::cross_join(     removed %>% dplyr::rename_with(~ paste0(.x, "_old")),     added %>% dplyr::rename_with(~ paste0(.x, "_new"))   )    candidates <- pairs %>%     dplyr::mutate(       similarity = name_sim(taxon_name_old, taxon_name_new),       same_species = same_val(Species_old, Species_new),       same_infraspecies = same_val(infraspecies_old, infraspecies_new),       same_author = same_val(taxon_authors_old, taxon_authors_new),       same_family = same_val(family_old, family_new),       same_year = same_val(year_actual_old, year_actual_new),              score = 0.40 * similarity +         0.25 * same_species +         0.10 * same_infraspecies +         0.15 * same_author +         0.05 * same_family +         0.05 * same_year     ) %>%     dplyr::filter(score >= 0.50)    if (nrow(candidates) == 0L) {     return(tibble::tibble())   }    candidates %>%     dplyr::mutate(       interpretacion = dplyr::case_when(         same_species & same_infraspecies & coalesce(infraspecific_rank_old != infraspecific_rank_new, FALSE) ~ "Posible cambio de rango",         same_species & coalesce(Genus_old != Genus_new, FALSE) ~ "Posible transferencia de genero",         similarity >= 0.85 & (same_author | same_year) ~ "Posible correccion ortografica",         TRUE ~ "Posible reemplazo taxonomico"       ),       puntuacion = round(score, 3)     ) %>%     dplyr::select(       exclusion_observada = taxon_name_old,       inclusion_observada = taxon_name_new,       familia = family_new,       interpretacion,       puntuacion     ) %>%     dplyr::arrange(dplyr::desc(puntuacion)) }  compare_versions <- function(old, new) {   removed <- dplyr::anti_join(old, new, by = "taxon_name")   added <- dplyr::anti_join(new, old, by = "taxon_name")   candidates <- candidate_replacements(removed, added)    linked_removed <- unique(candidates$exclusion_observada)   linked_added <- unique(candidates$inclusion_observada)    list(     summary = tibble::tibble(       version_anterior = unique(old$version),       version_nueva = unique(new$version),       registros_anteriores = nrow(old),       registros_nuevos = nrow(new),       inclusiones_observadas = nrow(added),       exclusiones_observadas = nrow(removed),       cambio_neto = registros_nuevos - registros_anteriores,       posibles_reemplazos = nrow(candidates)     ),     candidates = candidates,     probable_inclusions = added %>%       dplyr::filter(!taxon_name %in% linked_added) %>%       dplyr::select(taxon_name, family, year_actual),     probable_exclusions = removed %>%       dplyr::filter(!taxon_name %in% linked_removed) %>%       dplyr::select(taxon_name, family, year_actual)   ) }  comparison_14_15 <- compare_versions(ppendemic_tab14, ppendemic_tab15) comparison_15_16 <- compare_versions(ppendemic_tab15, ppendemic_tab16)

title: Cambios entre versiones de la base de plantas endemicas del Peru
text: Cambios observados y posibles reemplazos entre versiones. El cambio neto debe interpretarse junto con las inclusiones y exclusiones brutas. Por ejemplo, una correccion ortografica genera simultaneamente una salida y una entrada aunque represente al mismo taxon.
code: change_summary <- dplyr::bind_rows(   comparison_14_15$summary,   comparison_15_16$summary )  knitr::kable(   change_summary,   caption = "Cambios observados y posibles reemplazos entre versiones." ) plot_values <- rbind(   inclusiones = change_summary$inclusiones_observadas,   exclusiones = -change_summary$exclusiones_observadas,   cambio_neto = change_summary$cambio_neto )  barplot(   plot_values,   beside = TRUE,   names.arg = paste(     change_summary$version_anterior,     change_summary$version_nueva,     sep = " a "   ),   col = c("#2E8B57", "#B22222", "#4169E1"),   ylab = "Numero de registros",   legend.text = rownames(plot_values),   args.legend = list(x = "topleft", bty = "n") ) abline(h = 0, col = "grey40")

title: Cambios entre versiones de la base de plantas endemicas del Peru
text: Los siguientes pares deben contarse automaticamente como nuevas especies o perdidas de endemicidad. Comparten suficiente informacion para considerarlos posibles reemplazos entre versiones. Posibles reemplazos entre V-14 y V-15. Posibles reemplazos entre V-15 y V-16. Entre los patrones detectables se encuentran: cambios de terminacion, como Eudema chacasensis Eudema chacasense; correcciones ortograficas, como Senecio danal Senecio danai; cambios de rango, como Peperomia nivalis var. nivalis Peperomia nivalis f. nivalis; y posibles transferencias de genero, como especies de Lobivia que aparecen posteriormente bajo Echinopsis. Estos casos requieren validacion contra sinonimos, identificadores taxonomicos estables o la historia nomenclatural de WCVP.
code: knitr::kable(   comparison_14_15$candidates,   caption = "Posibles reemplazos entre V-14 y V-15." ) knitr::kable(   comparison_15_16$candidates,   caption = "Posibles reemplazos entre V-15 y V-16." )

title: Cambios entre versiones de la base de plantas endemicas del Peru
text: Despues de retirar los candidatos reemplazo, los nombres restantes son las inclusiones con mayor probabilidad de representar incorporaciones la lista. La version V-16 concentra numerosas inclusiones publicadas recientemente. Familias con mas posibles inclusiones entre V-15 y V-16. Ejemplos de posibles inclusiones publicadas desde 2024.
code: inclusions_15_16 <- comparison_15_16$probable_inclusions  inclusion_families <- inclusions_15_16 %>%   dplyr::count(family, name = "posibles_inclusiones") %>%   dplyr::arrange(dplyr::desc(posibles_inclusiones)) %>%   dplyr::slice_head(n = 15) %>%   dplyr::rename(familia = family)  knitr::kable(   inclusion_families,   caption = "Familias con mas posibles inclusiones entre V-15 y V-16." ) recent_inclusions <- inclusions_15_16 %>%   dplyr::filter(!is.na(year_actual), year_actual >= 2024) %>%   dplyr::arrange(dplyr::desc(year_actual)) %>%   dplyr::slice_head(n = 25)  knitr::kable(   recent_inclusions,   caption = "Ejemplos de posibles inclusiones publicadas desde 2024." )

title: Cambios entre versiones de la base de plantas endemicas del Peru
text: Los nombres retirados que tienen un reemplazo candidato son posibles exclusiones. Una exclusion puede deberse que el taxon dejo de considerarse aceptado o exclusivamente peruano, pero esa causa puede determinarse solo con estas tablas. Familias de las posibles exclusiones entre V-15 y V-16. Posibles exclusiones entre V-15 y V-16.
code: exclusions_15_16 <- comparison_15_16$probable_exclusions  exclusion_families <- exclusions_15_16 %>%   dplyr::count(family, name = "posibles_exclusiones") %>%   dplyr::arrange(dplyr::desc(posibles_exclusiones)) %>%   dplyr::rename(familia = family)  knitr::kable(   exclusion_families,   caption = "Familias de las posibles exclusiones entre V-15 y V-16." ) knitr::kable(   exclusions_15_16 %>% dplyr::arrange(family),   caption = "Posibles exclusiones entre V-15 y V-16." )

title: Cambios entre versiones de la base de plantas endemicas del Peru
text: El balance por familia ayuda identificar grupos que requieren una revision prioritaria. Este balance incluye tanto cambios taxonomicos como posibles incorporaciones o exclusiones reales. Mayores cambios absolutos por familia entre V-15 y V-16.
code: family_change <- dplyr::full_join(   ppendemic_tab15 %>% dplyr::count(family, name = "v15"),   ppendemic_tab16 %>% dplyr::count(family, name = "v16"),   by = "family" ) %>%   dplyr::mutate(     v15 = tidyr::replace_na(v15, 0L),     v16 = tidyr::replace_na(v16, 0L),     change = v16 - v15   ) %>%   dplyr::arrange(dplyr::desc(abs(change)), family)  knitr::kable(   head(family_change, 20),   caption = "Mayores cambios absolutos por familia entre V-15 y V-16." )

title: Cambios entre versiones de la base de plantas endemicas del Peru
text: Usar taxon_name para describir entradas y salidas observadas. Revisar primero los posibles reemplazos antes de comunicar descubrimientos o perdidas. Confirmar las posibles inclusiones y exclusiones con WCVP y sus identificadores estables. interpretar una exclusion como perdida biologica o extincion. Reportar siempre las versiones y fechas comparadas. El procedimiento presentado es reproducible y sirve como filtro inicial. Una evaluacion taxonomica definitiva requiere fuentes externas que documenten sinonimias, cambios de distribucion y decisiones nomenclaturales.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Paul E. Santos Andrade. Author, maintainer. Lucely L. Vilca Bustamante. Author.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Santos-Andrade PE, Vilca-Bustamante LL (2026). ppendemic: Accepted Endemic Plant Taxa Peru. R package version 0.2.2. Current data release based WCVP version 16, extracted 04 June 2026. https://paulesantos.github.io/ppendemic/. doi:10.5281/zenodo.5106619.
code: @Manual{,   title = {ppendemic: Accepted Endemic Plant Taxa from Peru},   author = {Paul E. {Santos Andrade} and Lucely L. {Vilca Bustamante}},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.2.2. Current data release is based on WCVP version 16, extracted 04 June 2026.},   doi = {10.5281/zenodo.5106619},   url = {https://paulesantos.github.io/ppendemic/}, }

title: A Glimpse at the Diversity of Peru's Endemic Plants
text: ppendemic provides access accepted endemic plant taxonomic records Peru. current dataset, ppendemic_tab16, contains 8030 records WCVP V-16, including taxa species infraspecific ranks. package useful : exploring taxonomic composition Peru’s endemic flora; checking whether submitted plant name listed endemic; comparing successive WCVP-derived versions endemic checklist; inspecting bibliographic metadata, including actual nominal publication years. database includes infraspecific taxa, number rows must interpreted number distinct species.

title: A Glimpse at the Diversity of Peru's Endemic Plants
text: datasets include accepted endemic taxa : accepted taxon name taxonomic status; family, genus, specific epithet, infraspecific rank infraspecific epithet; taxon authorship publication metadata; actual nominal publication years; dataset version extraction date. datasets include coordinates, departments, elevation, habitat, threat categories, fine-scale distribution records. Geographic hotspot, conservation-priority, range-size analyses require joining ppendemic external occurrence conservation datasets.

title: A Glimpse at the Diversity of Peru's Endemic Plants
text: ppendemic_tab16 latest dataset used default unless reproducible analysis requires older WCVP version. latest version expands previously known list 5,507 species presented Red Book Endemic Plants Peru 8030 accepted taxonomic records. records span 165 families; record-rich families Orchidaceae, Asteraceae, Piperaceae, Fabaceae, Bromeliaceae, Solanaceae, Melastomataceae, Araceae, Cactaceae, Rubiaceae.

title: A Glimpse at the Diversity of Peru's Endemic Plants
text: Install CRAN release: install development version GitHub:
code: install.packages("ppendemic") pak::pak("PaulESantos/ppendemic")

title: A Glimpse at the Diversity of Peru's Endemic Plants
text: Access latest dataset directly: Count records family:
code: library(ppendemic) #> ── Access Peruvian plant endemic data ─────────────────────── ppendemic 0.2.2 ── data("ppendemic_tab16") dplyr::glimpse(ppendemic_tab16) #> Rows: 8,030 #> Columns: 18 #> $ taxon_name           <chr> "Varronia lippioides", "Varronia munda", "Varroni… #> $ taxon_status         <chr> "Accepted", "Accepted", "Accepted", "Accepted", "… #> $ family               <chr> "Boraginaceae", "Boraginaceae", "Boraginaceae", "… #> $ Genus                <chr> "Varronia", "Varronia", "Varronia", "Adelonema", … #> $ Species              <chr> "lippioides", "munda", "vargasii", "orientale", "… #> $ infraspecific_rank   <chr> NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, N… #> $ infraspecies         <chr> NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, N… #> $ taxon_authors        <chr> "(I.M.Johnst.) J.S.Mill.", "(I.M.Johnst.) J.S.Mil… #> $ primary_author       <chr> "J.S.Mill.", "J.S.Mill.", "J.S.Mill.", "Croat", "… #> $ place_of_publication <chr> "Brittonia", "Brittonia", "Brittonia", "Syst. Bot… #> $ volume_and_page      <chr> " 65: 342", " 65: 343", " 65: 343", " 41: 39", " … #> $ first_published      <chr> "(2013)", "(2013)", "(2013)", "(2016)", "(2016)",… #> $ year_actual          <dbl> 2013, 2013, 2013, 2016, 2016, 1991, 2016, 2015, 2… #> $ year_nominal         <dbl> 2013, 2013, 2013, 2016, 2016, 1991, 2016, 2015, 2… #> $ both_years           <chr> "2013", "2013", "2013", "2016", "2016", "1991", "… #> $ has_different_years  <lgl> FALSE, FALSE, FALSE, FALSE, FALSE, FALSE, FALSE, … #> $ version              <chr> "V-16", "V-16", "V-16", "V-16", "V-16", "V-16", "… #> $ version_date         <chr> "04-06-2026", "04-06-2026", "04-06-2026", "04-06-… ppendemic_tab16 |>   dplyr::count(family, sort = TRUE) #> # A tibble: 165 × 2 #>    family              n #>    <chr>           <int> #>  1 Orchidaceae      1168 #>  2 Asteraceae        883 #>  3 Piperaceae        680 #>  4 Fabaceae          334 #>  5 Bromeliaceae      319 #>  6 Solanaceae        314 #>  7 Melastomataceae   263 #>  8 Araceae           214 #>  9 Cactaceae         188 #> 10 Rubiaceae         176 #> # ℹ 155 more rows

title: A Glimpse at the Diversity of Peru's Endemic Plants
text: Use is_ppendemic() check whether submitted names listed endemic: matching engine uses exact fuzzy matching. max_dist argument controls fuzzy tolerance: Indeterminate names sp. spp. treated genus-level records classified endemic taxa: is_ppendemic() can also used inside tabular workflows: detailed matching diagnostics, use matching_ppendemic():
code: splist <- c(   "Aa aurantiaca",   "Aa aurantiaaia",   "Werneria nubigena",   "Dasyphyllum brasiliense var. barnadesioides",   "Miconia firma",   "Festuca densiflora" )  is_ppendemic(splist) #> [1] "Endemic"     "Endemic"     "Not endemic" "Endemic"     "Endemic"     #> [6] "Endemic" is_ppendemic("Aa aurantiaaia", max_dist = 0) #> [1] "Not endemic" is_ppendemic("Aa aurantiaaia", max_dist = 2) #> [1] "Endemic" is_ppendemic(c("Piper sp.", "Piper spp.")) #> [1] "Not endemic" "Not endemic" tibble::tibble(splist = splist) |>   dplyr::mutate(endemic = is_ppendemic(splist)) #> # A tibble: 6 × 2 #>   splist                                      endemic     #>   <chr>                                       <chr>       #> 1 Aa aurantiaca                               Endemic     #> 2 Aa aurantiaaia                              Endemic     #> 3 Werneria nubigena                           Not endemic #> 4 Dasyphyllum brasiliense var. barnadesioides Endemic     #> 5 Miconia firma                               Endemic     #> 6 Festuca densiflora                          Endemic matching_ppendemic(splist) |>   dplyr::select(Orig.Name, Matched.Name, Endemic.Tag, matched) #>                                     Orig.Name #> 1                               Aa aurantiaca #> 2                              Aa aurantiaaia #> 3                           Werneria nubigena #> 4 Dasyphyllum brasiliense var. barnadesioides #> 5                               Miconia firma #> 6                          Festuca densiflora #>                                  Matched.Name Endemic.Tag matched #> 1                               Aa aurantiaca     Endemic    TRUE #> 2                               Aa aurantiaca     Endemic    TRUE #> 3                                    Werneria Not endemic   FALSE #> 4 Dasyphyllum brasiliense var. barnadesioides     Endemic    TRUE #> 5                               Miconia firma     Endemic    TRUE #> 6                          Festuca densiflora     Endemic    TRUE

Defaults "ambiguous_genera.csv".

title: Match Species Names to Endemic Plant List of Peru — matching_ppendemic
text: function first attempts directly match species names exact matches database (genus specific epithet, genus, specific epithet, infra species). exact match found, function performs fuzzy match using fuzzyjoin package optimal string alignment distance implemented stringdist. maximum edit distance can controlled max_dist. function matching_ppendemic returns tibble new columns Matched.Genus, Matched.Species, Matched.Infraspecies, containing matched names NA match found. Additionally, logical column added function called, allowing users see functions applied name matching process. process column shows NA, corresponding function called name already matched preceding function.

title: Match Species Names to Endemic Plant List of Peru — matching_ppendemic
code: # \donttest{ matching_ppendemic(c("Aa aurantiaca", "Aa aurantiaaia", "Werneria nubigena")) #>   sorter         Orig.Name  Matched.Name Endemic.Tag Orig.Genus Orig.Species #> 1      1     Aa aurantiaca Aa aurantiaca     Endemic         AA   AURANTIACA #> 2      2    Aa aurantiaaia Aa aurantiaca     Endemic         AA  AURANTIAAIA #> 3      3 Werneria nubigena      Werneria Not endemic   WERNERIA     NUBIGENA #>   Orig.Infraspecies Rank Infra.Rank Comp.Rank Matched.Genus Matched.Species #> 1              <NA>    2       <NA>      TRUE            AA      AURANTIACA #> 2              <NA>    2       <NA>      TRUE            AA      AURANTIACA #> 3              <NA>    2       <NA>     FALSE      WERNERIA            <NA> #>   Matched.Infraspecies Matched.Rank matched direct_match genus_match #> 1                 <NA>            2    TRUE         TRUE          NA #> 2                 <NA>            2    TRUE        FALSE        TRUE #> 3                 <NA>            1   FALSE        FALSE        TRUE #>   fuzzy_match_genus direct_match_species_within_genus #> 1                NA                                NA #> 2                NA                             FALSE #> 3                NA                             FALSE #>   suffix_match_species_within_genus fuzzy_match_species_within_genus #> 1                                NA                               NA #> 2                             FALSE                             TRUE #> 3                             FALSE                            FALSE #>   fuzzy_genus_dist fuzzy_species_dist Author #> 1               NA                 NA        #> 2               NA                  2        #> 3               NA                 NA        # }

title: ppendemic_tab14: Endemic Plant Database of Peru — ppendemic_tab14
text: ppendemic_tab14 dataset tibble (data frame) provides easy access comprehensive database Peru's accepted endemic plant taxa. contains 7,898 taxonomic records species infraspecific ranks, information including accepted name, family, genus, specific epithet, taxon authors, primary author, place publication, volume page, publication years, version details.

title: ppendemic_tab14: Endemic Plant Database of Peru — ppendemic_tab14
text: tibble (data frame) 7,898 rows 18 columns: taxon_name Character vector. accepted name endemic plant taxon. taxon_status Character vector. taxonomic status name (e.g., "Accepted"). family Character vector. family accepted name endemic plant taxon. Genus Character vector. genus endemic plant taxon. Species Character vector. specific epithet endemic plant taxon. infraspecific_rank Character vector. infraspecific rank (e.g., "subsp.", "var.") applicable. infraspecies Character vector. infraspecific epithet applicable. taxon_authors Character vector. author(s) accepted name endemic plant taxon. primary_author Character vector. primary author(s) publication containing endemic plant taxon information. place_of_publication Character vector. place publication endemic plant taxon information. volume_and_page Character vector. volume page number publication containing endemic plant taxon information. first_published Character vector. first published year publication containing endemic plant taxon information. year_actual Numeric vector. actual year publication extracted first_published. year_nominal Numeric vector. nominal year publication extracted first_published. both_years Character vector. actual nominal years different, extracted first_published. has_different_years Logical vector. Indicates whether actual nominal publication years differ (TRUE both_years contains pattern "YYYY|YYYY"). version Character vector. version identifier "V-14" ppendemic database. version_date Character vector. version date "28-05-2025" indicating version created.

title: ppendemic_tab14: Endemic Plant Database of Peru — ppendemic_tab14
text: dataset carefully compiled updated offer latest insights Peru's endemic plant taxa. data sourced World Checklist Vascular Plants (WCVP) database, international collaborative programme initiated 1988 Rafaël Govaerts provides high-quality expert-reviewed taxonomic data vascular plants. detailed methodology, see Govaerts et al. (2021) "World Checklist Vascular Plants, continuously updated resource exploring global plant diversity" Nature Scientific Data.

title: ppendemic_tab14: Endemic Plant Database of Peru — ppendemic_tab14
text: dataset provides curated collection Peru's accepted endemic plant taxa, gathered reputable botanical sources publications. data database extracted compiled World Checklist Vascular Plants (WCVP) database, comprehensive reliable repository botanical information. version (ppendemic_tab14) includes enhanced temporal information separate numeric fields actual nominal publication years. allows precise bibliographic tracking citation accuracy. dataset also includes improved infraspecific taxonomy handling dedicated fields ranks epithets. year extraction process uses sophisticated pattern matching distinguish actual publication years nominal years, has_different_years field automatically flagging records differ. particularly important historical botanical publications publication delays common.

View first few rows head(ppendemic_tab14) #> # A tibble: 6 × 18 #>   taxon_name   taxon_status family Genus Species infraspecific_rank infraspecies #>   <chr>        <chr>        <chr>  <chr> <chr>   <chr>              <chr>        #> 1 Pappobolus … Accepted     Aster… Papp… verbes… NA                 NA           #> 2 Maxillaria … Accepted     Orchi… Maxi… briggi… NA                 NA           #> 3 Pappobolus … Accepted     Aster… Papp… lanatus NA                 NA           #> 4 Maxillaria … Accepted     Orchi… Maxi… becken… NA                 NA           #> 5 Oreocereus … Accepted     Cacta… Oreo… doelzi… subsp.             calvus       #> 6 Hyptis salv… Accepted     Lamia… Hypt… salvio… NA                 NA           #> #   has_different_years <lgl>, version <chr>, version_date <chr>  # Check for taxa with different actual and nominal years different_years <- subset(ppendemic_tab14, has_different_years == TRUE) nrow(different_years) #> [1] 160  # what: ppendemic_tab15: Endemic Plant Database of Peru (based on WCVP v15) — ppendemic_tab15
title: ppendemic_tab15: Endemic Plant Database of Peru (based on WCVP v15) — ppendemic_tab15
text: ppendemic_tab15 dataset tibble (data frame) providing curated taxonomically validated list vascular plant taxa occur exclusively Peru. dataset derived version 15 World Checklist Vascular Plants (WCVP), facilitated Royal Botanic Gardens, Kew, corresponds extraction performed 06 January 2026.

title: ppendemic_tab15: Endemic Plant Database of Peru (based on WCVP v15) — ppendemic_tab15
code: ppendemic_tab15

title: ppendemic_tab15: Endemic Plant Database of Peru (based on WCVP v15) — ppendemic_tab15
text: tibble (data frame) 7,892 rows 18 columns: taxon_name Character vector. full scientific name accepted endemic taxon, constructed genus, species , applicable, infraspecific epithets, following WCVP standards. taxon_status Character vector. taxonomic status name according WCVP (e.g., "Accepted"). family Character vector. botanical family endemic taxon belongs, following WCVP family circumscription. Genus Character vector. genus name endemic taxon. Species Character vector. specific epithet endemic taxon. infraspecific_rank Character vector. infraspecific rank (e.g., "subsp.", "var.") applicable. infraspecies Character vector. infraspecific epithet applicable. taxon_authors Character vector. standardized authorship taxon name, concatenating parenthetical primary authors following botanical nomenclature conventions. primary_author Character vector. author(s) validly published scientific name. place_of_publication Character vector. journal, book publication taxon name effectively published. volume_and_page Character vector. volume page reference original publication taxon name (e.g., "13(2): 99"). first_published Character vector. year publication name, enclosed parentheses, reported WCVP. year_actual Numeric vector. actual year publication extracted first_published. year_nominal Numeric vector. nominal year publication extracted first_published. both_years Character vector. concatenation actual nominal years differ (format "YYYY|YYYY"). has_different_years Logical vector. Indicates whether actual nominal publication years differ. version Character vector. database version identifier "V-15", corresponding WCVP version 15. version_date Character vector. extraction date WCVP source dataset ("06-01-2026").

title: ppendemic_tab15: Endemic Plant Database of Peru (based on WCVP v15) — ppendemic_tab15
text: Data derived : Govaerts, R. (ed.). 2026. WCVP: World Checklist Vascular Plants. Facilitated Royal Botanic Gardens, Kew. Version 15. Extracted 06 January 2026. https://doi.org/10.34885/rvc3-4d77 methodological details: Govaerts, R., Nic Lughadha, E., et al. (2021). World Checklist Vascular Plants, continuously updated resource exploring global plant diversity. Scientific Data, 8, 215. https://doi.org/10.1038/s41597-021-00997-6

title: ppendemic_tab15: Endemic Plant Database of Peru (based on WCVP v15) — ppendemic_tab15
text: database contains accepted endemic taxon names, following taxonomic backbone WCVP filtered geographic distribution records indicating occurrence restricted Peru. dataset constructed World Checklist Vascular Plants (WCVP) version 15, continuously updated global taxonomic resource curated Royal Botanic Gardens, Kew. taxa rank species accepted status considered. Geographic distribution fields WCVP used identify taxa whose native range restricted exclusively Peru. Introduced, cultivated, misapplied, synonymic unplaced names excluded. nomenclature, authorship, family assignment publication details strictly follow International Code Nomenclature algae, fungi plants (ICN) WCVP editorial standards. Temporal metadata processed extract nominal actual publication years, allowing detailed historical bibliographic analysis Peruvian endemic flora.

title: ppendemic_tab15: Endemic Plant Database of Peru (based on WCVP v15) — ppendemic_tab15
text: Royal Botanic Gardens, Kew (2026). World Checklist Vascular Plants (WCVP), Version 15. https://wcvp.science.kew.org
